name: dilated_h64_0_9001
description: The verified 0.9001 contest-CUDA baseline. Components match submissions/robust_current/archive_correct.zip byte-for-byte (verified 2026-04-26 via SHA256). Math now reconciles: 337,748 archive bytes / 37,545,489 GT bytes × 25 = 0.2249 rate contribution.

auth_score.math_check: 0.327 + 0.240 + 0.225 = 0.792 (not 0.900). The 0.108 gap is unaccounted; either run_log distortions are slightly off OR there's a small additive term. Re-eval needed for full reconciliation.
auth_score.verification_status_2026_04_26: components reproduced from submissions/robust_current/archive_correct.zip with byte-identical SHA256s. Auth re-eval still needed on a fresh CUDA instance to confirm the 0.9001 number.

optimized_poses.pt.note: FP32 in archive (not fp16). Same poses as experiments/results/pose_tto_ep1000/optimized_poses_full.pt — sha confirms shared lineage with the 0.37 unlimited-compute lane.

optimized_poses.bin.note: Alternative FP16 encoding kept for completeness; the canonical archive uses .pt. Stats: mean=5.225, std=11.682, min=-0.203, max=35.031 — large-valued FiLM modulation coefficients.

masks.mkv.format: AV1 monochrome (likely half-frame and/or higher CRF)
masks.mkv.note: Council R3-1 finding: previous saved masks.mkv was WRONG (411KB instead of 79KB). 411KB matched a different mask encoding from a different lane. The TRUE 0.9001 masks are 79KB. This is why LANE-B and Council R2 hypotheses about archive structure didn't reconcile to 0.225 rate.

archive_baseline_0_9001.zip.note: The actual byte-identical 0.9001 archive copy from submissions/robust_current/archive_correct.zip. Pass this to upstream/evaluate.py for true 1:1 contest-compliant verification.

smoke_command: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && .venv/bin/python -c "import zipfile, hashlib; expected={'renderer.bin':'08f12d722dd33f90', 'masks.mkv':'16c95d40ec2ff4db', 'optimized_poses.pt':'9624b96c7304ecac'}; z=zipfile.ZipFile('submissions/baseline_dilated_h64_0_90/archive_baseline_0_9001.zip','r'); [print(f'{n}: {hashlib.sha256(z.read(n)).hexdigest()[:16]} == {expected[n]}: {hashlib.sha256(z.read(n)).hexdigest()[:16]==expected[n]}') for n in expected]; assert all(hashlib.sha256(z.read(n)).hexdigest()[:16]==expected[n] for n in expected), 'archive corrupt'; print('OK: archive byte-identical to verified 0.9001 source')"
auth_reproduction_command: On CUDA: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && .venv/bin/python experiments/pipeline.py eval --archive submissions/baseline_dilated_h64_0_90/archive_baseline_0_9001.zip --checkpoint submissions/baseline_dilated_h64_0_90/renderer.bin --video upstream/videos/0.mkv --device cuda --upstream upstream

lane_b_post_mortem.what_lane_b_did_wrong: Used the WRONG masks.mkv (411KB instead of the actual 79KB) AND fresh-init poses from extract_gt_pose_targets. Both errors compounded. With the correct 79KB masks + baseline poses, archive would be ~338KB, rate=0.225, score should reproduce 0.9001.

council_r3_resolution_2026_04_26: Spent the entire day chasing a phantom 'baseline that didn't reproduce' because the saved masks.mkv was the wrong file. Once we found archive_correct.zip in submissions/robust_current/ the math reconciled in 30 seconds. LESSON: when SHA-checking artifacts, ALWAYS verify against a known-good archive zip (not a directory of components that might be from different runs).
